title: Wip/chrisc/ps 66855 upgrade checklist template page
author: ChristopherCurryCMS

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR migrates the **Checklist Templates** admin/settings experience from legacy ASP.NET WebForms controls to **Angular components**, introduces a new Angular **ChecklistTemplate** DEF page, and adds UI plumbing for **evidence-required** indicators (badges/banners/tooltips) in checklist item flows.

**Changes:**
- Replaces the WebForms Templates panel in `ChecklistsSettings.aspx` with an embedded Angular `<checklist-templates>` app and webpack bundle entry.
- Adds Angular Checklist Templates list UI (grid + toolbar + row actions) and introduces a new Angular ChecklistTemplate DEF page + API client for publish/unpublish/import.
- Extends shared UI infrastructure (tab badges/info messages, registered-grid custom action cells, tooltip directive behavior) and adds preliminary “mandated evidence” UI across checklist item views.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 69 out of 70 changed files in this pull request and generated 7 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 76 out of 77 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

<details>
<summary>Files not reviewed (1)</summary>

* **Core/Web Layer/WebUI/WebAdmin/ChecklistsSettings.aspx.designer.cs**: Language not supported

reviews.author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer[bot]

reviews.body: Unfortunately we have a bit of a clash on the templates list page: I have reimplemented all tile and list views, including checklist templates, in the branch lucretia/TileAndListViewToAngular.
reviews.author: kcostenbader

review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body:
`RequiredEvidenceCount`, `ProvidedEvidenceCount`, and `RequiresEvidence` are currently hard-coded (e.g., always returning 5 / 2 / true). This will force evidence-required UI/behavior for every checklist item and report incorrect counts. These values should be computed from the actual checklist/template configuration and linked evidence, or removed until the real implementation is available.
```suggestion
        public int ProvidedEvidenceCount { get; set; }

        [NonNull]
        [DoNotApplyFieldSecurity]
        [DoNotRecordHistory]
        public bool RequiresEvidence { get; set; }
```
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body:
`applyCommentsTabEvidenceFromFieldValues` currently formats the attachment-required details using hard-coded placeholder counts (`["1", "2", "2"]`). This will display incorrect requirements in the Comments tab banner. Please either compute the real counts (comments/uploads/links) from available field values/API results, or omit the info message until those counts are available.
```suggestion
            // Intentionally omit the info message until accurate attachment-type counts are available.
```
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: This component/service pair (`list-values-button` / `ListValuesEditService`) is not referenced anywhere in the codebase (no selector usage, no DI usage), so it adds dead code to the PR. Either wire it into the checklist-template items grid flow or remove it for now. Also, the rowData keys use PascalCase (`"ValueType"`, `"ListValues"`) whereas the rest of the checklist-template items code uses lowercase keys (e.g. `"valuetype"`, `"listvalues"`), which will break if this is later used without normalization.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: `ListValuesEditService` is currently unused (only referenced by the unused `list-values-button` component). If the grid memo editor approach is the intended UX, consider removing this service from the PR to keep the ChecklistTemplate items tab surface area minimal.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: `refreshData()` is called before anything subscribes to `templates$`, so this emission is effectively lost. Since `templates$` already does an initial fetch via `startWith(undefined)`, this call is redundant and can be removed (or move the explicit refresh after the subscription if it’s meant to force a second fetch).
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body:
The `.WithComments(...)` string has a small mistake: it lists `param 1` twice. The last one should be `param 2` (record links) to match the `{0} {1} {2}` placeholders.
```suggestion
            AddNonFieldLabel(SUBAREA, "requiredAttachmentDetail", "{0} Comment(s) {1} file upload(s) and {2} link(s)").WithComments("param 0: number of comments required, param 1: number of file uploads required, param 2: number of record links required");
```
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: Hardcoded for now until added to the back end
review_comments.author: ChristopherCurryCMS

review_comments.body: The new tab badge styles use CSS variables that don’t exist in Default.css (e.g. `--Green-Green-Dark`, `--Red-Red`, `--Neutral-White`) and rely on hex fallbacks. This breaks the project convention of sourcing colours exclusively from `App_Themes/Default/Default.css` variables. Please switch to the existing variables (e.g. `--green-green-dark`, `--red-red`, `--neutral-white`) and remove hex fallbacks.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: `--Trimble-Blue` isn’t defined in Default.css, and the `var(--Trimble-Blue, #0063A3)` fallback introduces a hardcoded hex colour. Use the existing Default.css colour variable for this blue (e.g. `--blue-blue`) and avoid hex fallbacks to stay consistent with the global theming approach.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: The new info banner hardcodes colours (`#DCEDF9`, `#0063A3`). Component CSS should use the global colour variables from Default.css (e.g. `--blue-blue-pale` and `--blue-blue`) so themes remain consistent and centrally adjustable.
review_comments.author: Copilot
Copilot

review_comments.body: The mandated-evidence badge uses non-existent CSS variables (`--Green-Green-Dark`, `--Red-Red`, `--Neutral-White`) with hex fallbacks. Replace these with the existing Default.css variables (e.g. `--green-green-dark`, `--red-red`, `--neutral-white`) and remove hex fallbacks to avoid hardcoded colours.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: `executePublishAction` uses raw `ajax()` instead of `APIBase`’s request execution helpers, which means it bypasses shared behavior like RedirectToURL handling and centralized error handling. If the endpoint must return non-200 2xx statuses, consider extending/adding an APIBase helper that can surface status codes while still applying RedirectToURL logic; otherwise publish/unpublish can fail silently when the session expires.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: `StatusTrackerModule.CancelStatusTracker()` here is likely redundant because `ChecklistTemplateSystem.executePublishAction()` already creates and cancels the status tracker for publish/unpublish. Double-cancelling can inadvertently cancel an unrelated tracker started after the API call completes. Consider centralizing tracker ownership (either only in the API system or only in the page service).
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: Same as above: publish/unpublish status trackers appear to be cancelled both in `ChecklistTemplateSystem` and again here. It’s safer to let the layer that creates the tracker be the only layer to cancel it, to avoid cancelling a different tracker accidentally.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: This changes the ChecklistTemplate DEF page name to an Angular route (`app/ChecklistTemplate`). There is still a legacy `ChecklistTemplate.aspx` page and backend URL mapping (e.g. `WebAppSupport.GetDEFPageName`) that returns the ASPX path. Please ensure all URL generators are aligned (or add a redirect from the ASPX to the Angular route) so different navigation paths don’t open different implementations of the same record type.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: The templates panel visibility check now only uses `CanViewRecord` and no longer verifies list access (previously done via `DataViewsController.CurrentListDefinition`). If a user can view a record but cannot view the underlying list definition, the Angular panel will still show and then error when `ListViewService` loads views/security. Consider reintroducing a list access check (e.g. via `CanViewList(...)` / list definition availability) before showing the Templates panel/menu.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: WIP - back end to be implemented
review_comments.author: ChristopherCurryCMS

review_comments.body: `onListValuesEditorClosed` does separate null/undefined checks (`typeof value !== "undefined" && value !== null`). This is harder to read and deviates from the project's preferred falsy/nullish checks. Please simplify this guard (e.g., use a single nullish check like `value != null` while still allowing an empty string).
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: `ChecklistTemplatePage` (extends `PageBaseEditor`) now contains substantial UI/state/business logic (publish/lock state, error banner state, etc.). In this codebase the recommended pattern is to keep the page class thin and move domain logic/state into a dedicated `*PageService` (injecting `PageService` there) so the page remains mostly lifecycle/override wiring. Consider moving the publish/lock state computation and related signals into `ChecklistTemplatePageService` and expose them to the page via injection.
review_comments.author: Copilot
Copilot

review_comments.body: `[customActionCellTemplates]="[ActionsColumn]"` will pass `[undefined]` until `ActionsColumn` is initialized (it’s set asynchronously in the `settings$` subscription). `RecordListViewComponent` assumes each entry is a valid `CustomActionCellTemplate` and will dereference it when generating columns, which can throw at runtime. Consider binding an empty array until `ActionsColumn` is defined (e.g., a computed that returns `ActionsColumn ? [ActionsColumn] : []`, or conditionally render `<record-list-view>` only once `ActionsColumn` is set).
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: `showCondition: (data) => ...` uses the parameter name `data`, which is one of the discouraged non-descriptive names in the TS guidelines. Consider renaming this parameter to something specific like `rowData` (or `record`) to make it clear what shape it has and to align with the project conventions.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: `.common-is-required` sets `font-family: "Open sans", sans-serif;`. The repo CSS guidelines call out `Open Sans` as redundant because the font is already set globally (Default.css). Consider removing the `font-family` override here so the component inherits the global typography.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: `.mandated-evidence-badge` uses `width: 20px` together with horizontal padding (`padding: 0 8px ...`). This will either overflow/clamp the badge text (e.g. `"2 / 5"`) or force layout issues because the rendered box becomes wider than 20px. Consider switching to `min-width: 20px` (matching `.tab-badge` styling) and letting the content/padding drive the final width, or removing the fixed width entirely.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: Simplified check to `if (this._editingRowId !== null && value != null && this._grid) {`
review_comments.author: ChristopherCurryCMS

review_comments.body: Created `ChecklistTemplatePageService`
review_comments.author: ChristopherCurryCMS

review_comments.body: Removed
review_comments.author: ChristopherCurryCMS

review_comments.body: Renamed
review_comments.author: ChristopherCurryCMS

review_comments.body: Pass empty array if `ActionColumns` is null
review_comments.author: ChristopherCurryCMS

review_comments.body: Updated logic to match original code
review_comments.author: ChristopherCurryCMS

review_comments.body: Use min width and verified working with long values
review_comments.author: ChristopherCurryCMS

review_comments.body: Inline styles were added for the custom action cell container. Project guidelines require moving inline styles from templates into the component stylesheet (use a CSS class and bind it here) to keep styling maintainable and consistent.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: The fallback label `"Group"` is user-visible and bypasses the nomenclature system. Other list-view toolbars use a nomenclature-provided fallback (e.g., TeamMembers components use `group.caption || this.nomenclature().group`). Consider adding a `Group` (or similar) entry to `ChecklistTemplatesNomenclature` and using it here instead of a hardcoded string.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: The memo editor title is hardcoded as `"List Values"`, which is user-visible and won’t be translated via the nomenclature system. Consider sourcing this caption from nomenclature (e.g., the `ChecklistTemplateItem.ListValues` field label) or the list-view column caption so it stays consistent and localizable.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: `tabInfoMessage$` is an observable holder but isn’t marked `readonly`. The project TypeScript rules require observable holders (`Observable`/`Subject`/`BehaviorSubject`) to be `readonly` to avoid accidental reassignment; please change this to `public readonly tabInfoMessage$ = ...`.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: now readonly
review_comments.author: ChristopherCurryCMS

review_comments.body: Now uses nomenclature
review_comments.author: ChristopherCurryCMS

review_comments.body: Added to css
review_comments.author: ChristopherCurryCMS

review_comments.body: Added to nomenclature
review_comments.author: ChristopherCurryCMS

review_comments.body: Added 'registered-grid-customAction-cell' css class
review_comments.author: ChristopherCurryCMS

review_comments.body: `executeAction` currently treats any non-200 response as an error, including standard success statuses like 204 (No Content) and 304 (Not Modified). With the new `treatNon200SuccessResponseAsData` flag, the comment indicates 204/304 should be excluded from the special body-return behavior, but the current implementation still routes them through `handleResponseError`, which will show global error UI. Consider treating 204/304 as successful responses (cancel the tracker and return `null`/empty data) and reserving `handleResponseError` for actual error statuses (>= 400) or explicit error cases.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body:
The new `ListValues` nomenclature key has an incomplete XML summary ("The title for"). Please complete the summary so it accurately describes the purpose of this key (e.g., label/title used for the list values editor) to avoid confusing/low-quality generated docs and tooling.
```suggestion
            /// The title for the list values editor
```
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: This stylesheet uses `::ng-deep` to target internal Modus button markup/classes (`.modus-btn`, `.Inverted`). This is a fragile vendor override and can break when the Modus component implementation changes. Prefer styling via exposed component inputs/CSS variables, or apply layout styles to a wrapper element without relying on deep selectors into third-party internals.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: This preserves current functionality
review_comments.author: ChristopherCurryCMS

review_comments.body: Removed button styles but kept generic invert style.
review_comments.author: ChristopherCurryCMS
